meta.title: Present Simple: Daily Routines
meta.description: A modern first lesson on habits, routines, and the basic present simple pattern.

blocks.title: Talk about real life, not right now
blocks.content: Use the present simple for routines, habits, and everyday facts. Think about things that are generally true: what you do every day, where someone works, when school starts.

blocks.question: Which sentence talks about a routine?
options: I eat lunch at 12 every day.
blocks.correct_answer: I eat lunch at 12 every day.
blocks.explanation: Present simple is the routine form here: I eat lunch at 12 every day.

options.label: She walk to work every day.
She walks to work every day.
She walking to work every day.

blocks.prompt: You want to describe a daily habit. What should you use?

blocks.explanation: Present simple uses the base verb for habits and routines.

blocks.question: Quick recap: which sentence is correct?
options: He go to the gym on Mondays.
He goes to the gym on Mondays.
He is go to the gym on Mondays.
blocks.correct_answer: He goes to the gym on Mondays.
blocks.explanation: With he, she, and it, add -s: he goes, she works, it starts.
